purpose: Fresh gold set: maintainer-ratified answer key over blind agent extraction (template v2, faceted) at current HEAD; supersedes the 0205b0d v1 proposal.

verdictDefinitions.accept: claim correctly extracted and all agent labels correct
verdictDefinitions.relabel: claim correctly extracted but one or more labels corrected (proof route, audience, epic, etc.; correction in note)
verdictDefinitions.not-a-claim: text should not have been extracted as a behavior claim
verdictDefinitions.badly-bounded: real claim but wrong excerpt boundary, wrong merge, or duplicate (detail in note)
verdictDefinitions.retire-source: extraction correct, but the maintainer removes the source sentence (claim leaves the gold set; source-edit recorded for a later doc pass + re-anchor)
verdictDefinitions.rewrite-source: extraction correct, but the maintainer simplifies/rewrites the source sentence (claim essence kept but trimmed; source-edit recorded)

entries.summary: Cautilus is a framework that covers discovering, evaluating, and improving agent behavior.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: D1-discovery covers the discover job, E1-evaluate covers the evaluate job, and I1-bounded-improvement covers the improve job named in the framework scope.

entries.proofRationale: The claim can be checked by reading current source, docs, or generated artifacts.
entries.note: R9/R16 framing: identity/category sentence whose 3-job content is fully decomposed into L4 (value), L5 (connection) and the D1/E1/I1 epics; no independent provable composition. APEX-the-epic (catalog userStory) is the DAG root and L4 is the headline claim realizing it, so L3 is not load-bearing. README lead prose stays; not separately graded.

entries.summary: Cautilus lets you pin the behavior that matters, prove it survives every change to prompts/skills/models, and improve it within explicit budgets, whether protecting an AGENTS.md, a single skill, a prompt, or a full agent loop.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: E1-evaluate supports proving behavior survives change, and I1-bounded-improvement supports improving it within explicit budgets.

entries.proofRationale: The claim depends on model, agent, prompt, skill, workflow, or behavior execution evidence.
entries.note: Load-bearing APEX value proposition; R8 umbrella, proof = one representative end-to-end composition scenario (cautilus-eval/dev-repo, needs-scenario). Labels correct.

entries.summary: The three jobs connect: discover declared claims worth proving from selected source docs, verify curated claims through bounded evaluation packets, and improve behavior once the proof surface is honest.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: D1-discovery is the discover job, E1-evaluate is the verify-through-bounded-packets job, and I1-bounded-improvement is the improve job, all of which this connection claim spans.

entries.proofRationale: The claim depends on model, agent, prompt, skill, workflow, or behavior execution evidence.
entries.note: Workflow-sequencing claim (improve only once the proof surface is honest); distinct from L4. cautilus-eval/needs-scenario correct.

entries.summary: Cautilus ships as a standalone binary plus Cautilus Agent that a host repo can install without copying another scaffold first.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: A1-orchestration is supported because the Cautilus Agent shipped alongside the binary is the workflow-owning surface.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: no-scaffold-copy install model = S1 epic differentiator; deterministic (structural install model).

entries.summary: Agents are first-class users of the product surface (design-principle claim).

agentLabels.edgeRationale: APEX is supported because keeping behavior honest across change depends on the agent-facing product surface this principle establishes.

entries.proofRationale: The claim can be checked by reading current source, docs, or generated artifacts.
entries.note: R9/R16 framing, consistent with L3 (card-APEX-b1-1): the agent-first principle is the umbrella decomposed into L8 (packets resume) and L135 (agent-facing interfaces); the A1 epic node (catalog userStory) carries the agent-first role, so L7 is not a graded headline claim. README prose stays. Was a v2 recall-probe target (FN1 principle-shape) -> recall win preserved (agent saw it); maintainer grades it framing.

entries.summary: Commands should emit durable packets with enough state for the next agent to resume, not only terminal prose for a human operator (design-principle claim).

agentLabels.edgeRationale: E1-evaluate is supported because durable resumable packets are the evaluation output surface, and APEX because resumable packets keep behavior auditable across change.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: proof relabel deterministic -> cautilus-eval (dev/skill): R6 precedent ruled this exact claim cautilus-eval — "enough state for the next agent to resume" is behavioral content, not a schema check; R12 enabler=behavior. agentLabels left as-extracted (deterministic) for grading; correction recorded here.

entries.summary: Cautilus installs as a machine-level binary, but its agent-facing surface is intentionally repo-local.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: M1-proven-on-itself is supported because checked-in, reproducible, reviewable, repo-owned evaluation behavior is what makes the product auditable on itself.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.

entries.summary: `cautilus version --verbose` reports local version provenance and is the quickest binary-only summary of the current product surface: who it is for, which normalization family or eval surface to start from, and which report fields matter when a run rejects.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: S2-readiness is the home because version/verbose reports setup and orientation; S1-install is supported because version provenance is part of the install/lifecycle surface.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: version --verbose reports local version provenance + quickest binary-only product summary; R13 explicit T3 example (version --verbose fields); deterministic.

entries.summary: Legacy Homebrew installs are not a supported update channel; they must be removed and reinstalled via install.sh rather than mixing channels.
entries.proofRationale: The claim can be checked by reading current source, docs, or generated artifacts.
entries.note: Same Homebrew-unsupported claim as README:47; retire both for consistency (sourceEdits records remove README:47 + cli:32).

entries.summary: `cautilus init adapter` generates a minimal repo-local adapter scaffold and `cautilus doctor adapter` resolves and validates the adapter.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: S1-install is the home because adapter scaffold generation is part of consumer setup; S2-readiness is supported because `doctor adapter` validates adapter readiness.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: init adapter scaffold + doctor adapter resolve/validate; adapter is the core host-owned config; deterministic.

entries.summary: Each starter kit ships a pre-filled `cautilus-adapter.yaml`, a canonical input fixture, and a README explaining what to replace next; starter placeholders are bootstrap help, not proof a real consumer workflow is wired.
entries.proofRationale: The claim can be checked by reading current source, docs, or generated artifacts.
entries.note: starter kits (pre-filled adapter + fixture + README) and the placeholder-not-proof boundary (behavioral boundary -> claim per R16); human-auditable.

entries.summary: `cautilus <subcommand> --help` exits 0 for the registered native command surface, including grouped topics such as `cautilus improve search --help`.
entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: --help exits 0 for the registered native command surface incl grouped topics; R13 explicit T3 example; deterministic CLI minutiae.

entries.summary: `cautilus init run` starts a fresh per-run subdirectory under one stable artifact root and pins it; `workspace start` defaults `--root` to `./.cautilus/runs/` (auto-created on first use) and prints `export CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR=<absolute runDir>`, with `--json` available for machine-readable payloads.
entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: init run / workspace start run-directory management (per-run subdir, CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR); run-setup command detail; deterministic.

entries.summary: `cautilus evaluate comparison prepare` prepares clean baseline and candidate git worktrees for a compare run, and `cautilus doctor artifacts prune` prunes older Cautilus artifact bundles by `--keep-last`.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: E2-scenarios-experiments is the home because comparison prepare sets up baseline/variant compare runs; E1-evaluate is supported because artifact pruning maintains the run-bundle surface produced by evaluation.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: OVER-MERGE of two unrelated commands: cautilus evaluate comparison prepare (E2 compare worktree prep) + cautilus doctor artifacts prune (artifact cleanup). Split into 2 separate claims. Curator: split; both T3 command-detail.

entries.summary: `cautilus discover scenarios normalize` accepts chatbot, skill, and workflow summary inputs and normalizes them into scenario-normalization packets.
entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: discover scenarios normalize accepts chatbot/skill/workflow inputs -> scenario-normalization packets (E2 capability); deterministic.

entries.summary: Every normalize command plus `cautilus evaluate observation` and `cautilus evaluate report build` accepts `--example-input`, printing a minimal valid packet to stdout that can be piped back into the same command so operators can inspect the expected shape without clicking into a fixture on GitHub; these example commands are also exposed under `normalizationFamilies[*].exampleInputCli` in `discover scenarios --json`.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: E2-scenarios-experiments is the home because normalize/scenario commands are scenario-eval surfaces; S2-readiness is supported because example-input inspection helps operators learn the expected packet shape during onboarding.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: --example-input prints a minimal valid packet pipeable back (+ normalizationFamilies[*].exampleInputCli); CLI minutiae; deterministic.

entries.summary: Cautilus exposes two top-level evaluation surfaces and four presets: `dev / repo` for repo work contracts (replacing the prior `cautilus instruction-surface` commands), `dev / skill` for checked-in or portable development skills, `app / chat` for multi-turn product conversation behavior, and `app / prompt` for single product input/output behavior.

agentLabels.edgeRationale: E1-evaluate is the home because these are the core evaluate presets; E2-scenarios-experiments is supported because app/chat and app/prompt presets drive scenario and product-behavior experiments.

entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: two top-level surfaces + four presets (dev/repo replacing instruction-surface, dev/skill, app/chat, app/prompt); eval surface/preset model; deterministic. Cross-doc dup with README:116.

entries.summary: `cautilus evaluate fixture` runs a checked-in `evaluation_input.v1` fixture through an adapter-owned runner and then evaluates the observed packet, recording a top-level `proof` object in `eval-observed.json` and `eval-summary.json` when runner readiness can identify the proof class or assessment state.
entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: evaluate fixture runs a checked-in fixture through an adapter-owned runner then evaluates the observed packet + records a top-level proof object; core eval command; deterministic.

entries.summary: `cautilus evaluate observation` evaluates an already-observed packet without launching the runner again.
entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: evaluate observation evaluates an already-observed packet without relaunching the runner; main eval command (README L17); deterministic.

entries.summary: `cautilus evaluate report build` builds a machine-readable evaluation report packet from explicit mode runs, answering what the first product-owned decision packet is for this candidate versus the baseline.
entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: evaluate report build builds a machine-readable evaluation report packet from explicit-mode runs (candidate vs baseline decision packet); deterministic.

entries.summary: A minimal valid report packet is available via `cautilus evaluate report build --example-input`, including the minimum `humanReviewFindings` shape `{ "severity": "concern", "message": "...", "path": "optional" }`.
entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
entries.note: report build --example-input prints a minimal valid report packet incl humanReviewFindings shape; CLI minutiae; deterministic.

entries.summary: `cautilus improve` is the improvement front door, to be used only after the claim and eval proof surface are explicit enough that an improvement loop has something honest to improve against.
entries.proofRationale: The claim can be checked by reading current source, docs, or generated artifacts.

entries.summary: The bounded one-shot improver runs via `cautilus improve prepare-input` and `cautilus improve propose`.
entries.proofRationale: The claim names a deterministic gate or static contract that should be protected outside Cautilus eval.
